latest.name: Claude Opus 4.8
latest.headline: Anthropic's most capable model yet — better coding, reasoning, and honesty.
highlights: Agentic coding score up from 64.3% to 69.2%
~4× less likely to let a code flaw pass without comment
Fast mode runs at 2.5× speed and 3× cheaper than before
Dynamic workflows: hundreds of parallel subagents in Claude Code

timeline.title: UST is bringing Claude to physical AI
timeline.summary: Anthropic is an AI safety and research company that's working to build reliable, interpretable, and steerable AI systems.

timeline.title: A new way to reflect on how you use Claude
timeline.summary: Introducing a new way to reflect on and refine how you use Claude. It lets you easily track and visualize how you use Claude, and decide whether that time aligns with your goals.

timeline.title: The Long-Term Benefit Trust
timeline.summary: Anthropic is an AI safety and research company that's working to build reliable, interpretable, and steerable AI systems.

timeline.title: Inviting hard questions
timeline.summary: We're asking the public for their hardest questions about AI, and committing to show our work as we address them.

timeline.title: Ben Bernanke appointed to Anthropic’s Long-Term Benefit Trust
timeline.summary: Anthropic is an AI safety and research company that's working to build reliable, interpretable, and steerable AI systems.

timeline.title: Charting a path to AI accountability
timeline.summary: Anthropic is an AI safety and research company that's working to build reliable, interpretable, and steerable AI systems.

timeline.title: Frontier threats red teaming for AI safety
timeline.summary: Anthropic is an AI safety and research company that's working to build reliable, interpretable, and steerable AI systems.

timeline.title: Golden Gate Claude
timeline.summary: When we turn up the strength of the “Golden Gate Bridge” feature, Claude’s responses begin to focus on the Golden Gate Bridge. For a short time, we’re making this model available for everyone to interact with.

timeline.title: Progress from our Frontier Red Team
timeline.summary: In this post, we are sharing what we have learned about the trajectory of potential national security risks from frontier AI models, along with some of our thoughts about challenges and best practices in evaluating these risks.

timeline.title: Anthropic's core views on AI safety
timeline.summary: AI progress may lead to transformative AI systems in the next decade, but we do not yet understand how to make such systems safe and aligned with human values. In response, we are pursuing a variety of research directions aimed at better understanding, evaluating, and aligning AI systems.

timeline.title: Building safeguards for Claude
timeline.summary: Anthropic is an AI safety and research company that's working to build reliable, interpretable, and steerable AI systems.

timeline.title: Government of Alberta uses Claude to find and fix cybersecurity vulnerabilities
timeline.summary: The Government of Alberta has been using Claude Code with both Opus and Sonnet models to review its systems, find vulnerabilities, and fix them.

timeline.title: Announcing Anthropic's Responsible Scaling Policy
timeline.summary: We’re publishing our Responsible Scaling Policy—a series of technical and organizational protocols that we’re adopting to help us manage the risks of developing increasingly capable AI systems.

timeline.title: Claude's extended thinking
timeline.summary: Discussing Claude's new thought process

timeline.title: More details on Fable 5’s cyber safeguards and our jailbreak framework
timeline.summary: What is and isn't blocked by our cyber classifiers, and a first draft of our jailbreak severity framework

timeline.title: Redeploying Claude Fable 5
timeline.summary: Anthropic is redeploying Claude Fable 5 starting July 1 following the lifting of export controls, with updated cybersecurity safeguards and a new industry jailbreak framework.

timeline.title: Claude Fable 5 and Claude Mythos 5
timeline.summary: Today we’re launching Claude Fable 5: a Mythos-class model that we’ve made safe for general use.

timeline.title: Introducing Claude Sonnet 5
timeline.summary: Our most agentic Sonnet yet, with top-tier intelligence for coding and everyday professional work.

timeline.title: Claude Science, an AI workbench for scientists
timeline.summary: Claude Science is a customizable app that integrates the tools and packages researchers most often use, produces auditable artifacts, and provides flexible access to computing resources.

timeline.title: DXC integrates Claude into systems regulated industries rely on
timeline.summary: Anthropic is announcing a multi-year global alliance with DXC Technology, one of the world’s largest IT services companies.

upcoming.title: Claude Opus 4.9 / 5.0
upcoming.summary: Expected next flagship step. Watch for larger effort-control range and deeper agent orchestration.

upcoming.title: Expanded dynamic workflows
upcoming.summary: Parallel subagent orchestration expanding beyond Enterprise/Team/Max in Claude Code.

upcoming.title: Longer context tier
upcoming.summary: Competitive pressure from 2M-token rivals; a larger context option is plausible.

models.name: Claude Opus 4.8
models.note: Best-in-class agentic coding and the most reliable at flagging its own mistakes. The reference point for this comparison.

models.name: GPT-5.5
models.note: Strongest all-rounder; broad ecosystem and tooling. Slightly pricier on input than Claude.

models.name: Gemini 3.1 Pro
models.note: Leads published reasoning benchmarks and is the fastest of the group. Cheapest output among the frontier tier.

models.name: Grok 4
models.note: Largest context window at 2M tokens; strong raw coding scores.

models.name: DeepSeek V4-Pro
models.note: By far the cheapest frontier option — open-weight MoE, ~1.6T params. Great cost-to-performance.

models.name: Llama 4 Maverick
models.note: Open weights, self-hostable. Lower benchmark scores but unbeatable on price and control.

models.name: Mistral Large 3
models.note: European, strong on efficiency and multilingual tasks. Smaller context window than rivals.

samples.text: Opus 4.8 is genuinely the best coding model I've used — caught a race condition Cursor missed.
samples.label: positive

samples.text: Love that 4.8 flags its own uncertainty now instead of bluffing. Huge for trust.
samples.label: positive

samples.text: Fast mode on 4.8 still feels slow for me and the pricing stings vs Gemini.
samples.label: negative

samples.text: Solid bump but honestly nothing mind-blowing over 4.7 for my workflow.
samples.label: neutral

benchmarks.note: SWE-bench Pro (coding) and a blended reasoning index (GPQA/ARC-AGI). Figures are approximate, sourced from public leaderboards; see Sources. Lower is better for price.

metrics.label: Reasoning index
metrics.desc: Blended GPQA Diamond / ARC-AGI style reasoning score.

metrics.label: Output speed
metrics.desc: Generation throughput in tokens per second.

metrics.label: Output price
metrics.desc: Cost per million output tokens (lower is better).
